title: carex novae-angliae var. deflexa
intro: Carex novae-angliae var. deflexa, commonly known as the New England sedge, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. The therapeutic actions of Carex novae-angliae var. deflexa include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory actions, which can aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other polyphenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.

side_effects: Carex novae-angliae var. deflexa can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high fiber content.
